what_it_does: Stores YAML frontmatter metadata for an image attachment tied to a channel-memory entry in the design-source channel.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content.
The target is an image:// URI pointing to design-source/recent-pitches/p-26.
It records an added timestamp in ISO 8601 format.
The filename uses the att-<uuid>.md pattern.
standouts: Attachment metadata for an image asset linked to pitch p-26 in the design-source channel.
Use of an image:// URI scheme for the target.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
Follows the att-<uuid>.md naming convention for attachments.
References assets with a custom image:// URI.
concerns: The image asset has no caption, alt text, MIME type, or dimensions in this file.
The image:// target cannot be verified from the file alone.
No author or provenance information is recorded.
notes: A minimal, timestamped attachment record that points an image asset at a design-source pitch entry.
questions: What is pitch p-26 and where is its primary channel-memory entry?
Is the image stored locally or served remotely?
Who added this attachment and why?
What is the image format, size, or content?
purpose_vs_actual: The file's fields collectively describe an image attachment, which matches the attachment_type and target values; there is no explicit stated purpose text, but the metadata is consistent with cataloging an attached image.
intention_signals: It appears intended to register an image asset as an attachment to a design-source channel entry so the image can be discovered alongside the related pitch or design record.
novelty: The image:// URI scheme for asset targets is a notable convention in this file.
contribution: Provides a typed, timestamped link between a channel-memory entry and an image asset, giving downstream fusion a discoverable attachment record without needing to inspect the asset itself.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment record named att-c525fc6a.md in the design-source channel that links to image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-26 and records an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog an image attachment for a recent pitch entry in the design-source channel memory.
